---
title: "Hello, Python"
subtitle: "An example Quarto post with executable Python code."
date: "2026-05-15"
categories: [python, example]
format:
  html:
    code-fold: false
jupyter: python3
---

This post is a `.qmd` file with **Python code chunks**. Quarto runs the
code at render time and inlines the output (numbers, tables, plots) into
the page. To start a new post, copy this folder and edit `index.qmd`.

## A tiny dataset

```{python}
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "month": pd.date_range("2026-01-01", periods=6, freq="MS"),
    "revenue": [120, 138, 155, 162, 180, 205],
    "channel": ["organic"] * 6,
})
df
```

## A quick plot

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 3.5))
ax.plot(df["month"], df["revenue"], marker="o")
ax.set_title("Monthly revenue, organic channel")
ax.set_ylabel("Revenue ($k)")
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Notes

- Use `jupyter: python3` in the YAML header to enable Python execution.
- Heavy outputs (model training, big queries) get cached on first render
  via `freeze: auto` in [_quarto.yml](../../_quarto.yml) — only the chunks
  you change get re-executed.
